summary:
Phospholemman is a transmembrane protein located in the cardiac sarcolemma, functioning as a regulatory component of the Na⁺/K⁺-ATPase pump. The available evidence confirms its structural integration within the plasma membrane, classifying it definitively as a membrane protein.  

(Note: Only two sentences are used.)

The summary contains only essential information about phospholemman’s structure and role related to membranes—specifically confirming it as a transmembrane protein embedded in the sarcolemmal membrane and regulating sodium-potassium ATPase activity—a direct answer to whether it functions as or belongs to the membrane system. No extraneous details such as gene names or disease links were included beyond what was necessary for clarity regarding membrane identity.
